mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz A200d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned A200d engine typically costs £2,000–£7,000 supply only depending on generation. OM651-era engines (W176) sit at the lower end (£2,000–£5,500), while OM654 units (W177/V177) run £2,500–£7,000. A replacement OM654 engine costs on average £3,592 in good condition. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on workshop location. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz A200d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine generation your A200d has. The OM651 (W176, 2012–2018) is fundamentally reliable — 200,000–300,000 km examples are common — but has predictable weak points: timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km is the single most critical failure point. Early pre-2012 versions also suffered from Delphi piezo injector failures. The OM654 (W177/V177, 2018–present) is a more refined, lighter and generally more reliable engine. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz A200d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on the OM651 (W176) — the most critical failure; early Delphi piezo injector failure on pre-2012 OM651 units; EGR valve and DPF clogging on both OM651 and OM654; water pump leaks and cooling system issues. The OM651 timing chain is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement an engine-out job.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz A200d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold: if your A-Class is worth £8,000–£15,000 and a recon engine is £4,000 (27–50%), it's worth it. If the vehicle is a tired £6,000 example, the same engine exceeds 50% — get a scrap valuation first. For OM654-engined cars (W177, 2018+), higher vehicle values make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz A200d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: W176 (2012–2018) is OM651 (136 PS), W177/V177 (2018–present) is OM654 (150 PS). The A200d replaced the earlier A200 CDI model. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM651 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic failure requiring a full engine replacement (£2,000–£5,500+). The chain is located at the rear of the engine, making this an engine-out job. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles) significantly extend chain life.

eraMap.title: The A200d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: 2.1L four-cylinder diesel — timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km
2.0L four-cylinder diesel — more refined with improved chain design

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates. A replacement OM654 engine costs on average £3,592 in good condition, with reconditioned units up to £3,722. Dealer charges for engine replacement are typically £4,000–£7,000. Older OM651 units generally sit at the lower end of the range.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an A200d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The transverse engine layout in the A-Class requires more labour than longitudinal installations in rear-wheel-drive Mercedes models. The timing chain is located at the rear of the OM651 engine, making replacement labour-intensive.

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (OM651 — W176 2012–2018)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light, cam/crank correlation codes (P0016/P0017), poor performance. The chain stretches and the tensioners wear over time.
problems.typicalMileage: 120,000–160,000 km (~75,000–100,000 miles) — timing chain stretch is the single most critical failure point.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (chain, tensioners, guides). Replacing the chain is technically complex and costly, as it requires removing numerous components.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; if the chain jumps a tooth, it can cause catastrophic engine damage.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The OM651's timing chain is its single most critical failure point. If it stretches beyond tolerance or a guide fails, the consequences range from misfires and cam/crank correlation codes to catastrophic engine damage if the chain jumps a tooth. The chain is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement an engine-out job. At the first sign of rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles) significantly extend chain life.

problems.title: Early Delphi Piezo Injector Failure (OM651 — Pre-2012)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfires, decreased performance, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, fuel smell in the oil. Early Delphi piezo injectors are the most common failure point on pre-2012 OM651 engines.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (injector replacement). Post-2012 engines used magnetic solenoid injectors which are significantly more durable.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, misfires can damage catalytic converters.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The early Delphi piezo injectors fitted to pre-2012 OM651 engines are sensitive to fuel quality and operating conditions. They can cause erratic fuel delivery, rough running, and higher consumption. If you experience rough idle or misfires, have the injectors inspected. Post-2012 OM651 engines received more durable magnetic solenoid injectors.

problems.title: EGR Valve & DPF Clogging (OM651 & OM654)
problems.symptoms: Reduced performance, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, limp mode, hesitation between 1,500–2,500 rpm. Soot builds inside the intake and EGR passages, causing inconsistent airflow.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — accelerated by short urban journeys.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (EGR clean/replacement or DPF forced regeneration/clean). EGR valve replacement is often around £400–£800.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, DPF clogging can damage the turbocharger.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if DPF or EGR warnings appear.

problems.recommendation: Both the OM651 and OM654 are sensitive to EGR and DPF clogging, particularly with short urban journeys. The ECU compares requested vs measured air mass — when airflow becomes inconsistent, the car can enter limp mode. Regular motorway runs to allow DPF regeneration are essential. If you see EGR or DPF warnings, have the system inspected promptly.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Replace affected injectors — upgrade to magnetic solenoid type
Clean or replace — do it when warnings appear
Forced regeneration or clean — do it when warning appears
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This A200d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2018 OM654 with the updated timing chain design — more refined and reliable
* OM651 (W176) with full service history and regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* Post-2012 OM651 with the more durable magnetic solenoid injectors
* Full service history with regular DPF-friendly driving (motorway miles)
avoidIf: * OM651 with cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch is imminent
* Pre-2012 OM651 with rough idle or misfires — early Delphi injector failure
* No service history on any generation — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* DPF warning light or evidence of DPF issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The A200d badge spans two generations of Mercedes diesel. The OM651 (W176, 2012–2018) is fundamentally reliable but has predictable weak points — timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km is the single most critical issue. The OM654 (W177/V177, 2018–present) is a more refined and reliable unit, with improved chain design and 150 PS output. With the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold, a £4,000–£5,000 reconditioned engine on an A-Class valued at £8,000–£15,000 is 27–50% of the car's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. On a sub-£6,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare A200d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz A200d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (OM651 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (OM651/OM654) and oil-interval discipline
* Injector History — For pre-2012 OM651, check if Delphi injectors have been replaced
* DPF Status — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys accelerate clogging
* EGR System — Any hesitation between 1,500–2,500 rpm? (EGR clogging)
* Smoke Check — Black smoke could indicate injector or DPF issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
